back_translation: And the war was long between the house of Saul and the house of David; and David grew stronger and stronger, and the house of Saul grew weaker and weaker.
And sons were born to David in Hebron; and his firstborn was Amnon, by Ahinoam the Jezreelitess.
And his second was Chileab, by Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite; and the third was Absalom son of Maacah daughter of Talmai king of Geshur.
And the fourth was Adonijah son of Haggith; and the fifth was Shephatiah son of Abital.
And the sixth was Ithream, by Eglah David's wife. These were born to David in Hebron.
And it happened, during the war between the house of Saul and the house of David, that Abner was strengthening himself in the house of Saul.
And Saul had a concubine, and her name was Rizpah daughter of Aiah. And he said to Abner, 'Why did you go in to the concubine of my father?'

And Abner was very angry over the words of Ish-bosheth, and said, 'A dog's head am I that belongs to Judah? Today I do steadfast love with the house of Saul your father, with his brothers and with his friend, and have not delivered you into the hand of David — and you charge upon me guilt over the woman today.
Thus may God do to Abner and thus may He add to him, if as the LORD has sworn to David, so I will not do for him —
to transfer the kingdom from the house of Saul, and to establish the throne of David over Israel and over Judah, from Dan even to Beersheba.'
And he could no longer return a word to Abner, because of his fear of him.
And Abner sent messengers to David in his stead, saying, 'To whom belongs the land?' saying, 'Make your covenant with me, and behold, my hand is with you to bring around to you all Israel.'

And he said, 'Good. I will make a covenant with you. Only one thing I ask from you, saying, You shall not see my face unless before your bringing Michal daughter of Saul when you come to see my face.'
And David sent messengers to Ish-bosheth son of Saul, saying, 'Give me my wife Michal, whom I betrothed to myself with a hundred foreskins of the Philistines.'
And Ish-bosheth sent and took her from with her husband, from with Paltiel son of Laish.
And her husband went with her, going and weeping after her, as far as Bahurim. And Abner said to him, 'Go, return.' And he returned.
And the word of Abner was with the elders of Israel, saying, 'Both yesterday and the day before, you were seeking David as king over you.
And now do it, because the LORD has spoken to David, saying, By the hand of David my servant I will save my people Israel from the hand of the Philistines and from the hand of all their enemies.'
And Abner also spoke in the ears of Benjamin; and Abner also went to speak in the ears of David in Hebron all that was good in the eyes of Israel and in the eyes of all the house of Benjamin.
And Abner came to David at Hebron, and with him twenty men; and David made a feast for Abner and for the men who were with him.

And Abner said to David, 'Let me arise and go, and let me gather to my lord the king all Israel, and they shall make a covenant with you, and you shall reign over all that your soul desires.' And David sent Abner away, and he went in peace.
And behold, the servants of David and Joab came from a raid, and brought much plunder with them; and Abner was not with David in Hebron, for he had sent him away, and he had gone in peace.
And Joab and all the army that was with him came, and they told Joab, saying, 'Abner son of Ner came to the king, and he sent him away, and he went in peace.'
And Joab came to the king and said, 'What have you done? Behold, Abner came to you. Why is this — you sent him away, and he went, going?
You know Abner son of Ner, that to deceive you he came, and to know your going out and your coming in, and to know all that you are doing.'
And Joab went out from with David, and sent messengers after Abner, and they brought him back from the well of Sirah; but David did not know.
And Abner returned to Hebron, and Joab took him aside into the midst of the gate to speak with him privately, and struck him there in the belly, and he died for the blood of Asahel his brother.
And David heard afterward, and said, 'I am clean, and my kingdom from with the LORD forever, from the blood of Abner son of Ner.

May it whirl over the head of Joab, and over all the house of his father; and let there not be cut off from the house of Joab one with a discharge or with skin disease, or one who holds the staff, or one falling by the sword, or one lacking bread.'
And Joab and Abishai his brother killed Abner, because he had put to death Asahel their brother in Gibeon in the battle.
And David said to Joab and to all the people who were with him, 'Tear your clothes, and gird on sackcloth, and lament before Abner.' And King David walked behind the bier.
And they buried Abner in Hebron, and the king lifted up his voice and wept at the tomb of Abner, and all the people wept.
And the king lamented over Abner and said, 'Should Abner die as a fool dies?
Your hands were not bound, and your feet were not put into bronze fetters; as one falls before sons of wickedness, you fell.' And all the people again wept over him.

And all the people came to feed David bread while it was yet day; and David swore, saying, 'Thus may God do to me, and thus may He add, if before the going of the sun I taste bread or anything at all.'
And all the people noticed, and it was good in their eyes; everything that the king did was good in the eyes of all the people.
And all the people and all Israel knew on that day, that it was not from the king to put to death Abner son of Ner.
And the king said to his servants, 'Do you not know that a prince and a great one has fallen this day in Israel?
And I am this day weak though anointed king, and these men, sons of Zeruiah, are too hard for me. May the LORD repay the doer of evil according to his evil.'
